description: Empirical eval of the router skill: outcome -> Crew Manifest JSON. Each case is graded by code (manifest validates + crew non-empty) and by an LLM judge against a per-case rubric. passThreshold 7/10.

cases.task: Add JWT authentication to the Express REST API, including tests.
cases.rubric: A good manifest routes this software task to an implementation provider plus a test author; a security-review dimension is a bonus. Crew providers should be real (not all inline). The plan should build the endpoint then test it. Penalize: content/author roles, missing tests, empty/all-inline crew.

cases.task: Audit the entire codebase for security vulnerabilities and remediate the findings.
cases.rubric: A good manifest fans out review dimensions including a security-review provider, consolidates findings, then fixes with a verify gate. Penalize: a single implement-only crew, no security dimension, no remediation/verify step.

cases.task: Write a 600-word launch blog post announcing our new analytics dashboard.
cases.rubric: A good manifest routes this content task to an authoring provider and ends with a humanize phase (strip AI-tells). Penalize: software/implement roles, code review, missing humanize.

cases.task: Diagnose and fix the failing unit test in the payments service.
cases.rubric: A good manifest is failure-first: reproduce/diagnose the failure before fixing, then verify the suite is green. Penalize: jumping to a broad feature build, no reproduction step, no verify.

cases.task: Refactor the legacy dateUtils module for readability without changing behavior.
cases.rubric: A good manifest routes to a refactor/simplification provider and includes a behavior-preservation check (tests stay green). Penalize: feature additions, behavior changes, no regression guard.

cases.task: Research and recommend a vector database for our RAG pipeline.
cases.rubric: A good manifest routes to a research provider that gathers and synthesizes evidence and ends with a cited recommendation. Penalize: jumping to implementation, no evidence/sources, code-heavy crew.
